description: Cross-platform QR code generator with helpers for Wi-Fi, SMS, mailto, geo, and tel. Default mode encodes a text payload from a positional argument or stdin.

platform.value_on_windows: Native gap-fill — Windows has no built-in QR generator; single-binary AOT avoids requiring Python or a Cygwin-built qrencode.
platform.value_on_unix: One tool covering unicode terminal output, ascii for no-UTF8 terminals, SVG and PNG, plus structured helpers (Wi-Fi / SMS / mailto / geo / tel) missing from qrencode.

options.description: Structured JSON metadata for AI agents
Allow PNG output to a TTY.
Overwrite an existing --output file (refused by default).
Output format: auto, unicode, ascii, svg, png. Default auto (unicode on TTY, svg when stdout redirected).
Error-correction level: l (~7%), m (~15%, default), q (~25%), h (~30%).
Write to file instead of stdout.
Coloured output: auto (default when omitted), always, or never

exit_codes.description: Usage error: bad flags, missing required field for a helper subcommand, helper grammar violation (e.g. tel/sms with letters), --format vs --output extension contradiction, refusing to overwrite an existing --output file without --force, PNG to TTY without --force-binary
Runtime error: payload exceeds QR capacity (try --error-correction l), stdin not valid UTF-8, output write failed (parent missing, permission denied, etc.)

io.stdin: Text payload to encode when no positional is given, or when '-' is passed.
io.stdout: Rendered QR code (unicode/ascii/svg by default; bytes for PNG unless --output).
io.stderr: Errors, capacity-overflow hints, and diagnostic messages.

examples.description: Encode text, render unicode QR to the terminal

composes_with.description: Pipe any text producer into qr for encoding
Encode the clipboard contents as a QR code
